title: Tenant Data Import Export Semantic Contract
description: Coverage-complete semantic contract for the tenant data import export capability. Semantic information captured as a USF coverage record via the coverage record and equivalence audit; no external source code is imported and the V2 implementation language is an open decision.
aiGuidance: Coverage-complete draft grounded in semantic information (USF coverage record only; no source import; language open). Proof is hermetic/stale lineage, not current readiness. Enrich detail and publish fresh proof before generation; respect the hexagonal constraint in ADR 0005.

lifecycle.description: Provides a paired tenant data export and import flow with portability integrity, producing an encrypted compressed archive on export and applying it on import through a Postgres applier, with durable resumable progress so a failed transfer can resume rather than restart. The overriding invariant is that a failed transfer must never cause silent data loss. Import progress is persisted so partial work survives interruption.

stateModel.description: Durable import progress is keyed by tenant/organisation and the archive's content digest and tracks completed units plus an optional failed unit, persisted by a dedicated migration; it deliberately stores no payload or secret material. The transported payload is an encrypted compressed archive built on export and consumed on import; the canonical tenant data itself lives in the relational store and is read or written through repository ports. Progress is idempotent against re-runs of the same archive digest.

permissions.description: Import and export are tenant-scoped, security-sensitive operations that must preserve tenant isolation so a transfer cannot cross tenant boundaries. The operation is attributable and audited, and the durable progress record carries only tenant and digest keys rather than tenant content, limiting exposure. Permission, audit, and fail-closed behaviour are explicitly required to be preserved.

contracts.description: The contract is an export/import route pair: export emits a portable encrypted archive of tenant data and import applies it with resumable, idempotent progress keyed by tenant and archive digest. The applier must guarantee that an interrupted import resumes from recorded progress without duplicating completed units and without silently dropping failed ones. Recovery must preserve tenant boundary, audit, and data-subject evidence.

validation.description: Import validates the archive digest to key progress and detect re-application, validates archive integrity (encrypted, well-formed) before applying, and records a failed unit rather than discarding it. Missing or invalid configuration fails closed; the progress record's tenant and digest keys are required to resume. Re-running the same archive is validated as idempotent against recorded completed units.

errorModel.description: The model is fail-closed and explicitly designed so that failed transfers never silently lose data: a failed unit is recorded, completed units are preserved for resume, and provider (archive builder or applier) failure returns a typed degraded or readiness error with mutation boundaries closed. Tenant data cannot be silently dropped or remapped; incident class is tenant-data-or-security with explicit tenant-data-loss risk if export/import semantics are bypassed.

auditModel.description: Operations are tenant-scoped and audited through typed error logs carrying the tenant/organisation identifier and route/use-case/provider spans that preserve tenant context, while durable progress records make resumability and partial-failure state observable. The progress record intentionally excludes payload and secrets so the audit surface does not leak transported content. Recovery must preserve audit and data-subject evidence.

readinessModel.description: Readiness depends on the archive builder and the Postgres import applier being available; the capability degrades explicitly when those adapters are unavailable, disabling the transfer path while keeping read-only readiness and audit visible. Readiness being blocked, a failed portability proof during promotion, or a missing isolation or audit signal raise alerts and block promotion.

proof.description: Behaviour is exercised by the data-portability proof and portability unit tests covering resumable, idempotent import progress and no-silent-loss semantics. Proof is hermetic/runtime lineage; fresh USF proof pending USF-101/USF-59.

uiSemanticDefinition.description: No distinct UI capability at the contract level (coverage matrix ui=0); UI is modelled separately and remains a future AI-customised deliverable.
